title: Rampage of the Phlogs
content:

|timeline=[[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]]<ref name="Date note">{{InsiderCite|23|Star Wars Publications Timeline|''Star Wars'' Publications Timeline}} dates the events of the [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']] animated series, which include the events of "Rampage of the Phlogs," to three [[Standard year/Legends|years]] after the events of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|''Star Wars'': Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], which corresponds to [[3 ABY/Legends|3 ABY]], according to ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]''.</ref>

"'''Rampage of the Phlogs'''" is the third episode of the [[Ewoks: Season One|first season]] of [[Ewoks (television series)|''Ewoks'']]. Written by [[Paul Dini]] and directed by [[Raymond Jafelice]], the episode originally aired on [[ABC Television Network|ABC]] on [[September 21]], [[1985]].

==Official description==
''Morag separates a baby Phlog from his parents and blames the Ewoks, prompting an attack.''

==Plot summary==
===The phlog travelers===
[[Kneesaa a Jari Kintaka/Legends|Kneesaa]] and [[Latara/Legends|Latara]] are floating down a river when they are knocked over by a giant wave, capsizing their raft. The two [[Ewok/Legends|Ewoks]] look up to see a [[Phlog/Legends|Phlog]] making waves in the water with [[Hoom|his]] club. Behind the Phlog appears two more, a [[Gender/Legends|male]] and a female with a baby Phlog in her arms.

As the Phlogs travel down the river, Kneesaa and Latara return to [[Bright Tree Village/Legends|Bright Tree Village]] to warn the [[Council of Elders (Ewok)/Legends|Elders]] that the Phlogs have returned to the forest. As [[Wicket Wystri Warrick/Legends|Wicket]] listens intensely to the conversation, [[Malani]] appears behind him and flirtatiously tries to get his attention. Wicket, however, shows no interest, instead listening closely to [[Logray/Legends|Logray]], who warns the Ewoks to stay out of the Phlogs' way. While the Phlogs are peaceful, they are dangerous if disturbed.

===Morag's conspiracy===
Meanwhile, the Phlogs are resting by the river when the baby [[Nahkee]] wanders off. He encounters a [[Zakool]], which the young Phlog treats like a pet. When the Zakool runs off, the Phlog crawls after him. Instead of finding the Zakool, however, he is surrounded by [[Rakazzak beast/Legends|Rakazzak beasts]], which are controlled by [[Morag]] the [[Tulgah/Legends|Tulgah]] witch. Morag puts a spell on Nahkee, which puts him to sleep. The Rakazzak beasts carry Nahkee away, and Morag leaves behind a [[Bow/Legends|bow]], two [[Spear/Legends|spears]], and an Ewok hood. When the other three Phlogs search for Nahkee, they discover the items, and immediately assume that Nahkee was captured by Ewoks, much to Morag's delight. Morag and the Rakazzak beasts take Nahkee to the [[Dulok/Legends|Duloks]], who are ordered to care for him until the Phlogs destroy the Ewok village.

Back at the Ewok village, Malani is playing with a [[Munyip/Legends|Munyip]] when she sees Wicket, Kneesaa, Latara, and [[Teebo/Legends|Teebo]] climbing through the trees. Wicket begins remarking on how he "likes Malani far away from him," which Malani overhears. She runs off in distress. The Munyip tells Malani's brother Teebo, and the four Ewoks search the woods for Malani. Instead of Malani, however, the Ewoks find an enraged Phlog, who chases after the Ewoks. The Ewoks hide in a log, which rolls down a hill and shatters on a rock, sending the Ewoks flying into the river. Teebo and Wicket decide to continue their search, while Kneesaa and Latara return to the village to warn the Elders that the Phlogs are on a rampage.

[[Gorneesh/Legends|Gorneesh]], [[King/Legends|King]] of the Duloks, cares for the Phlog child, which consumes food at an alarming rate. Gorneesh grows tired of watching over the Phlog, and decides to tie his foot to a tree stump, giving Gorneesh a chance to rest. The young Phlog, however, simply pulls the stump from the ground while Gorneesh is sleeping. He then crawls away, dragging the stump behind him.

===The lost child===
Malani, who is in the woods alone in tears, hears crying something behind her. She turns around and sees the young Phlog, who has become immobilized, the stump he is dragging wedged between two trees. She unties the rope from the stump, and the Phlog tells her in his own primitive way that he has lost his parents. Teebo and Wicket finally find Malani, who is being carried by her new friend. Teebo believes that if the Phlog is brought back to his parents, they might leave the Ewoks alone. However, before they can act on this plan, Nahkee smells cooking food, and runs off in the direction of the scent. Teebo and Wicket grab onto the rope, which is still being dragged by the Phlog. The scent, however, leads the Phlog and the Ewoks back to the [[Dulok Swamp|Dulok village]]. Gorneesh is pleased to see that the Phlog has returned with three young Ewoks.

While the Ewok village is under siege from the three adult Phlogs, Logray explains to [[Chief/Legends|Chief]] [[Chirpa/Legends|Chirpa]] that the [[Dobah|mother Phlog]] claims the Ewoks stole her baby. Chirpa believes Morag is behind the crime, and Malani and Kneesaa set out to find the missing child. They are soon captured, however, and taken to the Dulok village, where the five young Ewoks care for the baby Phlog under the eye of King Gorneesh. While Wicket apologizes to Malani for the things he said, Teebo and Latara secretly concoct a plan for their escape.

===Reunion and victory===
Teebo enlists the help of nearby [[Humming peeper]]s, who help Kneesaa and Latara serenade the Duloks to sleep. The Ewoks, carrying the sleeping Nahkee, escape to the Ewok village, which is fighting a losing battle against the Phlogs as Morag looks on. The young Ewoks arrive and reunite Nahkee with his family, just in time to save their village from destruction. The Duloks are not far behind, but are forced to flee back to their village when they are attacked by angry Phlogs and an angrier Morag. Malani and Nahkee share a tender goodbye, while Chirpa makes peace with the mother and [[Zut|father Phlog]].

==Continuity==
This episode shows the return of the [[Phlog/Legends|Phlogs]], a species which was introduced in ''[[How the Ewoks Saved the Trees: An Old Ewok Legend]]'', albeit with a much different look.<ref name="How the Ewoks Saved the Trees: An Old Ewok Legend">''[[How the Ewoks Saved the Trees: An Old Ewok Legend]]''</ref> The Phlogs would also later appear in the first season episode entitled "[[Blue Harvest]]."<ref name="Blue Harvest">{{Ewoks|Blue Harvest}}</ref>

The character of Kneesa's sister [[Asha/Legends|Asha]] is first referenced but not seen in the episode. She is introduced on screen in the episode entitled "[[Asha (episode)|Asha]]".<ref name="Asha episode">{{Ewoks|Asha (episode)}}</ref> She is later mentioned in the season 2 episode entitled "[[The Raich]],"<ref name="The Raich">{{Ewoks|The Raich}}</ref> and she appears in the season 2 episodes entitled "[[Home Is Where the Shrieks Are|Home is Where the Shrieks Are]]," "[[Night of the Stranger]]," "[[Horville's Hut of Horrors (episode)|Horville's Hut of Horrors]]" and "[[Battle for the Sunstar]]."<ref name="Home Is Where the Shrieks Are">{{Ewoks|Home Is Where the Shrieks Are}}</ref><ref name="Night of the Stranger">{{Ewoks|Night of the Stranger}}</ref><ref name="Horville's Hut of Horrors episode">{{Ewoks|Horville's Hut of Horrors (episode)}}</ref><ref name="Battle for the Sunstar">{{Ewoks|Battle for the Sunstar}}</ref>

This episode was released on DVD in 2004 as part of the feature film [[The Haunted Village (film)|''The Haunted Village'']]. It can be found on side 1 of the disc.<ref name="The Haunted Village film">[[The Haunted Village (film)|''The Haunted Village'' film]]</ref>
